novel_features.name: Where Is My Stuff
novel_features.description: All in-flight Amazon shipments with their current status and ETA in one view.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining every open order with its shipments and live tracking; the official UI shows one order at a time and competitors output static CSVs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to answer 'is my package coming today' across many orders, this is the one-shot view.

novel_features.name: Delivery Slips Detector
novel_features.description: Orders whose actual delivery date slipped more than N days from the original estimate.
novel_features.rationale: Comparing original ETA to actual delivery requires historical snapshots only available because we sync periodically into SQLite.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces unreliable carriers and sellers without manually scrolling through every order.

novel_features.name: Spend Rollup
novel_features.description: Spending broken down by month, year, category, seller, or payment method.
novel_features.rationale: A SQL aggregate over orders + items + transactions; incumbents export static CSVs that downstream tools have to roll up themselves.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Gives agents a one-shot answer for budgeting, expense reporting, and trend analysis.

novel_features.name: Top Items
novel_features.description: Most-ordered items by frequency or by total spend, ASIN-grouped across all history.
novel_features.rationale: Group-by ASIN across years requires a normalized item store; existing tools only show per-order item lists.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Helps an agent reason about what the user actually consumes vs one-off purchases.

novel_features.name: Subscribe & Save Detector
novel_features.description: Recurring purchases inferred from order history (same ASIN ordered on a regular cadence).
novel_features.rationale: Heuristic detection over the local store; the official S&S page only lists currently-active subscriptions.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces candidates for actual S&S enrollment and detects de-facto subscriptions the user may not realize they have.

novel_features.name: Find That Thing
novel_features.description: FTS5 search across orders, items, sellers, and tracking notes.
novel_features.rationale: FTS5 needs the data ingested locally; live searching the Amazon UI is slow and shallow.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Direct answer to 'when did I order that thing' without scrolling through years of order history.

novel_features.name: Arriving Soon
novel_features.description: Shipments arriving in the next N days, sorted by ETA.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-order ETA window query needs the local store; existing tools show one order at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent plan around incoming deliveries (e.g. 'is my router arriving before the meeting on Friday?').

novel_features.name: Late Shipments
novel_features.description: Active shipments past their original estimated delivery date.
novel_features.rationale: Detecting late requires the original ETA snapshot, which only exists if we sync periodically into a store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces carrier delays the moment they happen, no manual review.

narrative.headline: Walk your Amazon order history offline — every order, item, shipment, and dollar in a local SQLite store no other tool gives you.
narrative.value_prop: Sync once and ask cross-cutting questions forever. Where is my stuff right now, what did I spend last quarter, which deliveries are slipping, when did I order that thing — answered in milliseconds without re-hitting the live site or burning agent context on full HTML pages.
narrative.auth_narrative: Amazon publishes no buyer API. The CLI imports cookies from your logged-in Chrome / Firefox / Safari / Brave session via `auth login --chrome`. Those cookies persist locally, refresh automatically, and authenticate every subsequent fetch — no API key, no OAuth, no resident browser at runtime.

quickstart.comment: Import cookies from your logged-in browser session — required for any authenticated fetch.
Walk the last 90 days of orders into the local SQLite store at a polite pace.
See every in-flight package with current status and ETA.
Roll up your 2026 Amazon spending by month.
Find every order containing 'usb-c cable' — FTS5-backed, instant, offline.

troubleshoots.fix: Make sure you're logged in to amazon.com in Chrome, then re-run `auth login --chrome --domain amazon.com`.
Pass `--rate 0.5` to slow the per-order detail fetch, or omit `--full-details` to fetch only the listing pages.

troubleshoots.symptom: Order detail returns 401 even when logged in
troubleshoots.fix: Amazon rotated your session-id; re-run `auth login --chrome` to refresh cookies.

troubleshoots.symptom: `track <id>` returns empty when the order has multiple shipments
Foreign-locale orders parse with garbled dates
troubleshoots.fix: v1 supports US (.com) only. Multi-region shipping in v2; track issue.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for amazon-orders-pp-cli when an agent needs to reason across the user's Amazon order history — multi-order tracking radars, recurring-purchase detection, spending rollups, FTS5 searches like 'when did I order that USB-C cable'. The local SQLite store means cross-cutting questions return instantly without re-hitting Amazon and without burning context on full HTML pages.

recipes.title: Where is everything I'm waiting on?
recipes.explanation: Lists every open shipment with current status and ETA — the unified radar across all in-flight Amazon orders.

recipes.title: How much did I spend this year?
recipes.explanation: Rolls up monthly Amazon spend from the local store; no live calls, sub-second.

recipes.title: Find the order with that thing I bought
recipes.explanation: FTS5 search across orders + items; uses dotted `--select` to extract just the fields the agent needs.

recipes.title: What ships am I being charged for that aren't here yet?
recipes.explanation: Shows active shipments past their original ETA so an agent can flag carrier delays.

recipes.title: What do I actually buy?
recipes.explanation: Groups every purchased ASIN across history by total spend — surfaces the recurring purchases.

trigger_phrases: what did I spend on Amazon this month
find that thing I ordered on Amazon
when did I order from Amazon
